Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 331 [Mul-Apin 2][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o i 1o i 1

* ina itiŠU U₄ 15-KAM mul⸢KAK⸣.SI.SA₂ IGI.[LA₂-ma]

(o i 1) In Du'uzu (IV), on the 15th day, the Arrow appears and the daytime watch is 4 minas, the [...] watch 2 minas.

o i 22

4 MA.NA EN.NUN u₄-mi 2 MA.NA EN.NUN [...]

o i 33

dUTU ša₂ ina id tu₁₅⸢SI.SA₂⸣ KI SAG.DU mulUR.GU.[LA] [...]

(o i 3) The Sun, which [...] northwards with the Lion's head, turns and keeps descending southwards at 40 nindanu a day.

o i 44

GUR-ma ana id tu₁₅⸢U₁₈.LU⸣ U₄ 40 NINDA-TA.AM₃ ul-ta-⸢nap⸣-[pal]

o i 55

U₄-ME LUGUD₂.⸢DA-MEŠ⸣ GE₆-MEŠ GID₂.DA-[MEŠ]

(o i 5) The days shorten. The nights lengthen.

o i 66

* ina itiDU₆ U₄ ⸢15⸣-KAM dUTU ina lib₃-bi mulzi-ba-ni-[tu₄] [(...)]

(o i 6) In Tašritu (VII), on the 15th day, the Sun rises in the middle of the Scales [...] and the Moon stands in front of the Bristle, behind the Hireling, and the daytime watch is 3 minas, the night-time watch 3 minas.

o i 7-87-8

KURḫa u d⸢30⸣ ina IGI MUL.MUL ⸢EGIR⸣ mulLU₂.ḪUN.GA₂  ; GUB-ma 3 ⸢MA⸣.NA EN.NUN u₄-mi 3 MA.NA EN.NUN GE₆

o i 9-109-10

* ⸢ina itiAB⸣ U₄ 15-KAM mulKAK.SI.SA₂ ina li-la₂-a!-ti  ; [IGI].LA₂-ma 2 MA.NA EN.NUN u₄-mi 4 MA.NA EN.[NUN] GE₆

(o i 9-10) In Ṭebetu (X), on the 15th day, the Arrow appears in the evening and the daytime watch is 2 minas, the night-time watch 4 minas.

o i 11-1311-13

dUTU ša₂ ina id tu₁₅U₁₈.LU KI SAG.DU ⸢mulUR.GU.LA KURḫa⸣  ; GUR-ma ana id tu₁₅SI.SA₂ U₄ 40 NINDA-TA.AM₃  ; un-⸢da⸣-na-ḫar U₄-MEŠ GID₂-MEŠ GE₆-⸢MEŠ⸣ LUGUD₂-MEŠ

(o i 11-13) The Sun, which rose southwards with the Lion's head, turns and keeps rising northwards at 40 nindanu a day. The days lengthen. The nights shorten.

o i 1414

* ina itiBARA₂ U₄ 15-⸢KAM⸣ d30 ina li-la-a-ti ina ŠA₃ mulzi-ba-[ni-tu₂ ...]

(o i 14) In Nisannu (I), on the 15th day, in the evening the Moon [...] in the middle of the Scales [...], and sunrise stands in front of the Bristle, behind the Hireling, so that 3 minas [...].

o i 1515

u dUTU.E₃.A ina IGI MUL.MUL ⸢EGIR⸣ mulḪUN.GA₂ GUB-ma ⸢3⸣ MA.NA [...]

o i 1616

* ina ⸢iti⸣BARA₂ U₄ 15-KAM ⸢itiŠU⸣ U₄ 15-KAM ina itiDU₆ U₄ 15-[KAM]

(o i 16) In Nisannu (I), on the 15th day; in Du'uzu (IV), on the 15th day; in Tašritu (VII), on the 15th day; in Ṭebetu (X), on the 15th day: you observe the risings of the Sun, the visibility times of the Moon, and the appearances of the Arrow. You will discover the excess days.

o i 1717

ina ⸢iti⸣AB U₄ 15-KAM KUR-MEŠ ša₂ dUTU NA-MEŠ ša₂ ⸢d30⸣ IGI.DU₈.A-[MEŠ]

o i 1818

ša₂ ⸢mul⸣KAK.SI.SA₂ URU₃ar₂ U₄-MEŠ DIRI-MEŠ IGI.LA₂

o i 1919

* ina ⸢itiKIN⸣ [...] 10-KAM mulERIDUki U₄ 15-KAM mulŠU.PA

(o i 19) In Ululu (VI), on the 10th [...] Eridu (appears), on the 15 day the ŠU.PA-constellation.

o i 2020

AN [...] ⸢MUL⸣-šu₂-nu ina u₄-mi ⸢IGI⸣-MEŠ zi-me-šu₂-nu

(o i 20) On the day their star appears you observe their risings, [...] that blows. [...] the horses [...] they drink [...] the river.

o i 2121

[...] ⸢ša₂⸣ DUku ⸢URU₃⸣ ANŠE.KUR.RA-MEŠ

o i 2222

[...] ⸢ID₂⸣ [...] NAG-MEŠ

o i 2323

ki-i MUL-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ul-ta-ta-pu-ni maḫ-ḫu-ri-šu₂-nu

(o i 23) When their stars have become visible [...] offerings to them. [...] bitumen; they drink water from the river.

o i 2424

[...] x x ⸢ESIR ina⸣ ID₂ A-MEŠ NAG-MEŠ

o i 2525

[...] ⸢U₄ 15-KAM mulGABA.GIR₂⸣.[TAB]

(o i 25) [...] on the 15th day the Scorpion's chest.

(4 lines missing)
o i 3131

[...] mulUDU.IDIM.SAG.UŠ

(o i 31) [...] Saturn [...] the Sun's star.

o i 3232

[...] MUL dUTU

o i 3333

[...] zi-⸢me⸣-šu₂-nu

(o i 33) [...] their appearances.

o i 34-3534-35

[... AN]⸢e⸣ TAG-ME  ; [...] ⸢zi⸣-me-šu₂-nu

(o i 34-35) They touch [...] of heaven. [...] you observe their risings [...]. They touch bitumen.

o i 3636

[...] URU₃

o i 3737

[...] ESIR TAG!-MEŠ

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
o ii 1'o ii 1'

[...] ⸢MUL.MUL⸣ [...]

(o ii 1') [...] the Bristle [...].

o ii 2'2'

[...] ⸢mul⸣KAK.SI.SA₂ ⸢ina li⸣-[la-a-ti ...]

(o ii 2') [...] the Arrow in the evening [...].

o ii 3'3'

[... tu₁₅U₁₈].⸢LU⸣ KURḫa GUR-ma [ana] ⸢id tu₁₅SI⸣.[SA₂ ...]

(o ii 3') [...] rises southwards, turns, and then [...] northwards.

o ii 4'4'

[...] mulKAK.SI.SA₂ [ina] ⸢li⸣-la-a-ti [...]

(o ii 4') [...] the Arrow in the evening [...].

o ii 5'5'

[...] U₄ 15-KAM mulKU₆ mulŠU.GI IGI.LA₂ ⸢MU BI⸣ [...]

(o ii 5') [...] on the 15th day, the Fish and the Old Man appear: that year [...].

o ii 6'6'

[...] ⸢iti⸣BARA₂ U₄ 15-⸢KAM⸣ mul⸢KU₆⸣ [mul]ŠU.⸢GI IGI-ME⸣ MU BI ⸢DIRI⸣[at₂]

(o ii 6') [In] Nisannu (I), on the 15th day, the Fish and the Old Man appear: that year is leap.

o ii 7'7'

[...] ⸢MUL⸣-MEŠ ša₂ šu-ut de₂-a šu-ut ⸢da-nu₃⸣ šu-ut ⸢dEN⸣.[LIL₂]

(o ii 7') [...] stars of Ea, of Anu, of Enlil.

o ii 8'8'

[...] AN u MU₂-MEŠ-šu₂-nu ⸢KIN⸣-[ma ...] ta-nab-⸢bi⸣

(o ii 8') You seek [...] and their risings, and then you name [...].

o ii 9'9'

[...] x MU.AN.NA tuš-ta-bal-ma 3 MU-⸢MEŠ⸣

(o ii 9') [...] you multiply the year and then you make a prediction for the 3rd year, and say [...] leap.

o ii 10'10'

⸢MEa GAR⸣-ma ⸢MU⸣ [... DIRI].GA ta-qab-bi

o ii 11'-12'11'-12'

⸢ana⸣ U₄.NA₂-AM₃ 12 ITI-MEŠ šu-ta-ku-lu  ; ina MU 3-KAM ITI DIRI.GA ta-qab-bi

(o ii 11'-12') To multiply the day of the Moon's disappearance for 12 months, you say the leap month for the 3rd year.

o ii 13'13'

ina 12 ITI-MEŠ 10 U₄-MEŠ DIRI-MEŠ ŠID MU.AN.NA

(o ii 13') In 12 months 10 leap days is the count for a year.

o ii 14'14'

šum₄-ma U₄.DA.ZALe u₄-⸢mi ITI⸣ u MU.AN.NA ana IGI-⸢ka⸣

(o ii 14') If you are to discover the intercalation of the day, month, and year, you multiply 0;01 40, the intercalation of a day, by a month, so that you discover 0;50, the intercalation of a month. You multiply 0;50, the intercalation of a month, by 12 months, so that you discover 10, the days [...] year. For the third year, you say that that year will be leap.

o ii 15'15'

1 40 U₄.DA.ZALe u₄-⸢mi⸣ ana ITI IL₂-ma

o ii 16'16'

50 U₄.DA.ZAL.LA₂ ITI ⸢IGI⸣ 50 U₄.DA.ZAL.⸢LA₂e ITI ana 12 ITI IL₂⸣-ma

o ii 17'17'

10 ⸢U₄-MEŠ⸣ [...] MU.AN.⸢NA⸣ ina MU 3-KAM MU DIRI.⸢GA ta⸣-qab-[bi]

o ii 18'18'

[...] 2-⸢KAM₂⸣ BAL dšul-⸢gi⸣

(o ii 18') [...] 2nd [...] Šulgi's reign.

o ii 19'19'

[... iti]⸢DIRI⸣.[ŠE].⸢KIN.KU₅⸣ BAL MAR.⸢TU₂⸣

(o ii 19') A 2nd Addaru (XII) (alludes to) the reign of the Amorites.

o ii 20'20'

[... iti]KIN.[d]⸢INANA 2-E⸣ BAL kaš-⸢ši⸣-[i]

(o ii 20') A 2nd Ululu (VI) (alludes to) the reign of the Kassites.

r i 1r i 1

(r i 1) [...] on the 15th day, the daytime watch is 3 minas, the night-time watch 3 minas.

r i 22

[...] ina 1 KUŠ₃ GISSU 2 1/2 ⸢DANNA u₄-mu⸣

(r i 2) [...] cubit shadow: 2 1/2 bēru of daytime.

r i 33

[...] ina 1 ⸢KUŠ₃ GISSU 1 DANNA 7⸣ UŠ ⸢30 NINDA⸣ u₄-⸢mu⸣

(r i 3) [...] cubit shadow: 1 bēru, 7 UŠ, 30 nindanu of daytime.

r i 44

[...] 1 ⸢KUŠ₃ GISSU 2/3⸣ [...] 5 ⸢UŠ⸣ u₄-mu

(r i 4) [...] cubit shadow: 2/3 [...], 5 UŠ of daytime.

r i 55

Column ii

r ii 1'r 1'

[...] GI [...]

(r ii 1') (traces)

r ii 2'2'

* ⸢iti⸣GAN itiAB iti[ZIZ₂ ...]

(r ii 2') Kislimu (IX), Ṭebetu (X), Šabaṭu (XI) [...] it looks earthward: diminution [...].

r ii 3'3'

⸢KI⸣ ina?-ṭal nu-uḫ-ḫu-[ut ...]

r ii 4'4'

* [...] ⸢ŠA₃ ni-di⸣ KURḫa 3!(4)[20 ...]

(r ii 4') [...] it rises inside a cumulus cloud: a king [...].

r ii 5'5'

* ⸢dUTU⸣ ina ŠA₃ ⸢ni⸣-di ŠU₂ [...]

(r ii 5') (If) the Sun sets inside a cumulus cloud: [...].

r ii 6'6'

* ⸢MUL TA tu₁₅MAR₃⸣ SUR-ma ana ŠA₃ [...]

(r ii 6') (If) a star flares up from the west and inside [...].

r ii 7'7'

* ⸢MUL TA tu₁₅⸣MAR₃ SUR-ma ana ŠA₃ [...]

(r ii 7') (If) a star flares up from the west and inside [...].

r ii 8'8'

[...] ⸢TA⸣ tu₁₅MAR₃ SUR-ma ana ŠA₃ [...]

(r ii 8') [...] flares up from the west and inside [...].

r ii 9'9'

[... 3-šu₂]-⸢nu⸣ E₃-ma [...]

(r ii 9') [...] emerges as three [...].

r ii 10'10'

[...] ⸢tu₁₅⸣KUR x tu₁₅⸢U₁₈ SUR-ma⸣ [...]

(r ii 10') [...] flares up [from] east to south [...] and then sets [in] the west: for 3 years the land [...].

r ii 11'11'

[...] ⸢tu₁₅MAR⸣ ŠU₂ MU ⸢3-KAM⸣ KUR [...]

r ii 12'12'

[...] ⸢tu₁₅⸣MAR ana ŠA₃ ⸢tu₁₅KUR⸣ DIB [...]

(r ii 12') [...] passes [from] west to the middle of the east: [...].

r ii 13'13'

[...] ⸢TA⸣ MURU₂ ANe SUR-⸢ma⸣ ina d⸢UTU⸣.[ŠU₂.A] ⸢ŠU₂ ZI.GA⸣ [...]

(r ii 13') [...] flares up from the middle of heaven and sets at sunset: loss [...].

r ii 14'14'

[*] mulGIR₂.TAB IGI ⸢tu₁₅U₁₈ DU MU⸣ BI SIG₅

(r ii 14') (If) the Scorpion appears (and) the south wind blows: that year will prosper.

r ii 15'15'

* ⸢MUL⸣ [...] LUḪ.ḪA MAN MAR SIG₅

(r ii 15') (If) a star [...]: the king of the west will prosper.

r ii 16'16'

* ⸢MUL dAMAR.UTU⸣ [...] MU.AN.NA ⸢IGI⸣ ina ⸢MU BI AB.SIN₂⸣ <<NIG₂>> SI.SA₂

(r ii 16') (If) Marduk's star appears [...] year: in that year the furrows will thrive.

r ii 17'17'

* ⸢MUL dAMAR⸣.UTU ⸢MUL.MUL⸣ KURud ina ⸢MU⸣ BI dIŠKUR ⸢RA⸣

(r ii 17') (If) Marduk's star reaches the Bristle: in that year Adad will devastate.

r ii 18'18'

* ⸢MUL⸣ dAMAR.UTU mulUGAmušen KURud ŠE.GIŠ.I₃ NIM GIŠ

(r ii 18') (If) Marduk's star reaches the Raven: early sesame will thrive.

r ii 19'19'

* ⸢MUL⸣ dAMAR.UTU pa-gar NA IGI be-en-nu DAB-su

(r ii 19') (If) Marduk's star sees a man's body: epilepsy will seize him.

r ii 20'20'

[... d]⸢AMAR⸣.UTU NA A ⸢ir-muk NAM⸣.TAG.⸢GA GAL₂⸣ši

(r ii 20') (If) a man bathes in water [...] Marduk's star: there will be guilt.

r ii 21'21'

* [x] ⸢d⸣AMAR.UTU GE₆-ma IGI.LA₂ ⸢MU⸣ BI ⸢A.SAG₃⸣ GAL₂ši

(r ii 21') (If) Marduk's [star] is dark when it appears: that year there will be asakku-illness.

r ii 22'22'

* ⸢mulŠUDUN⸣ ina E₃-⸢šu₂⸣ da-aʾ-im ILLU up-pu-<lu> E₃

(r ii 22') (If) the Yoke is dark when it emerges: late floods will emerge.

r ii 23'23'

* ⸢mul⸣[ŠUDUN] ⸢ina⸣ E₃-šu₂ ⸢it⸣-ta-na-an-paḫ ⸢ILLU ḫar-pu⸣

(r ii 23') (If) the Yoke keeps lighting up when it emerges: early flood.

r ii 24'24'

* ⸢mulŠUDUN ina E₃-šu₂ GIM IZI⸣ it-<ta>-na-an-⸢paḫ BURU₁₄ SI.SA₂⸣

(r ii 24') (If) the Yoke keeps lighting up like fire when it emerges: the harvest will thrive.

r ii 25'25'

* ⸢mulŠUDUN ina E₃-šu₂ šup⸣-[pul]-ma da-⸢aʾ-im ILLU NU⸣ GAL₂

(r ii 25') (If) the Yoke is low and dark when it emerges: there will not be floods.

r ii 26'26'

* ⸢mulŠUDUN⸣ [... d]⸢UTU⸣.ŠU₂.A IGI-šu₂ GARnu tu₁₅[MAR] ⸢ZI⸣-ma

(r ii 26') (If) the Yoke puts its face towards sunset [...], the west wind arises then turns to the south: in Ululu (VII), on the 10th day, ruination of the land will be brought about.

r ii 27'27'

ana tu₁₅U₁₈ NIGIN₂ ina ⸢itiKIN U₄⸣ 10-KAM ⸢šal-pu₂-ti KUR⸣ GAR

r ii 28'28'

* mul⸢ŠUDUN⸣ ina ⸢E₃-šu₂⸣ d⸢UTU.KU₄.KU₄⸣ IGI-šu₂ ⸢GAR⸣nu IGI ⸢AN⸣ [IGI].⸢BAR⸣-ma ⸢TU₁₅ mim-ma la i-zi⸣-[iq]

(r ii 28') (If) the Yoke puts it face towards sunset when it emerges and faces the front of heaven: whatever wind blows, hunger will be brought about.

r ii 29'29'

⸢SU⸣.GU₇ GARan

r ii 30'30'

⸢BAL NAM.GILIM⸣.MA ša₃ [...]-⸢d⸣30 ⸢LUGAL⸣ [ŠEŠ].⸢UNUGki⸣ ka-mu-us-su DU.DU

(r ii 30') Reign of annihilation of [Ibbi]-Sin, king of Ur, who went in bondage. [...] they weep and fall.

r ii 31'31'

x DU ⸢i⸣-[ba]-⸢ak-ku⸣-u u [i]-⸢ma⸣-aq-⸢qu₂⸣-tu

r ii 32'32'

* ⸢šum₄⸣-ma LU₂ [ana NUN]⸢ti⸣ GAR-⸢ma⸣ TU₁₅-1 DU BAL SIG₅

(r ii 32') If a man is pronounced for rulership and the south wind blows: the reign will prosper.

r ii 33'33'

* TU₁₅-2 DU NINDA ⸢SIG GU₇⸣ TU₁₅-3 ⸢DU⸣ U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ LUGUD₂.DA-[MEŠ]

(r ii 33') (If) the north wind blows: he will eat thin bread. (If) )the east wind blows: his days will be short.

r ii 34'34'

* TU₁₅-4 DU ⸢NU SI⸣.[SA₂]

(r ii 34') (If) the west wind blows: he will not thrive.

r ii 35'35'

[*] ⸢mulAPIN ZA₃.TIL.LA⸣.BI.[ŠE₃]

(r ii 35') Mul-Apin until its end.

r ii 36'36'

mdUTU—[x]—⸢eri⸣-ba x x x x [...]

(r ii 36') Šamaš-[...]-eriba, ....
